title: SMB
description: Server Message Block — file, printer, and resource sharing protocol for Windows networks.
fullExplanation: SMB (Server Message Block) is a network file sharing protocol that allows applications to read and write to files and request services from server programs. It runs directly over TCP port 445 (modern) or over NetBIOS on port 139 (legacy). SMB is the foundation of Windows file sharing, printer sharing, and named pipes. SMBv3 (Windows 8+) added encryption, multichannel support, and improved performance.
keyFacts: Used for Windows file and printer sharing
SMBv2 introduced in Windows Vista — reduced chattiness
CIFS is an older dialect of SMB

versions.feature: Windows Vista — fewer round trips, better performance
